lucidBlocksBeginnerGuide.title: Dragon Quest Smash Grow Reroll Guide
lucidBlocksBeginnerGuide.intro: Use the fast 26-pull route, claim the web store rewards, and lock a start that carries story progress.

steps.title: Rush the opening setup
steps.description: Install the game, create your avatar, and clear the opening quickly. You can rename and re-customize later, so speed matters most during reroll loops.

steps.title: Finish the tutorial draw first
steps.description: Clear the tutorial and take the guaranteed Steel Sword pull before judging account quality. Real reroll value starts after tutorial completion.

steps.title: Link Square Enix account before spending
steps.description: Set age, link account, and prepare the official web store claim flow so your reroll run unlocks all launch tickets.

steps.title: Claim the full 26-pull package
steps.description: Collect the free web store reward, reopen the game, then claim all presents for 20 ticket pulls plus 6 gem pulls in one cycle.

steps.title: Prioritize Metal series keeps
steps.description: Metal Wing Boomerang and Metal Slime Sword are top launch keeps because they accelerate early progression and metal farming value.

steps.title: Use a clear keep line
steps.description: Safe stop is one Metal-series 3-star weapon. Ideal stop is one Metal-series 3-star plus one additional 3-star before counting redraw.

steps.title: Finish with guaranteed redraw
steps.description: After you pass your keep line, secure a second core weapon from redraw. Shiverstick is the most universal pickup for stable story clears.

steps.title: Reset cleanly when pulls miss
steps.description: Use Home > Menu > Title > Data Management to delete a failed run and restart from character creation for the next loop.

quickTips: A full reroll loop averages around 13 minutes when route discipline is consistent.
Do not skip account linking if you want the complete 26-pull cycle every run.
Metal-series starts scale better than random off-focus 3-star openings.
Lock your keep line before you start to avoid wasting time on weak boxes.

lucidBlocksApotheosisCrafting.title: Dragon Quest Smash Grow Beginner Guide
lucidBlocksApotheosisCrafting.intro: Your first 1-2 hours should focus on unlock thresholds, role concentration, and efficient resource timing instead of random grinding.

cards.name: Lock reroll and redraw before real progress
cards.description: Early damage comes from your weapon box, so finalize reroll keeps and complete guaranteed redraw before long story sessions.

cards.name: Push story before side farming
cards.description: Story clears unlock core systems and party slots, making it the best early-time investment.

cards.name: Focus one main vocation first
cards.description: Avoid splitting levels too wide. One main role around Lv.30 keeps early clears consistent and stable.

cards.name: Raise several weapons to Lv.30
cards.description: Broad Lv.30 weapon coverage beats over-investing one piece and improves elemental answers.

cards.name: Use Training Quests at progression walls
cards.description: Target weapon stones, armor stones, gold, and Metal Slime EXP instead of low-value wandering stages.

cards.name: Unlock expedition idle rewards early
cards.description: After Chapter 1 Normal, start dispatching immediately so offline time keeps feeding upgrades.

cards.name: Use Adventure Rank 7 and 8 checkpoints
cards.description: Rank 7 unlocks vocation changes, and Rank 8 unlocks the Vocation Panel for long-term growth.

cards.name: Prioritize party-wide panel nodes
cards.description: Whole-team gains stretch early growth stones further than narrow single-character branches.

cards.name: Save Metal Tickets and clear Rookie Panel
cards.description: Delay ticket spending until your box is stronger and finish Rookie Panel missions for a guaranteed 3-star equipment ticket.

lucidBlocksToolsAndWeapons.title: Dragon Quest Smash Grow Best Weapons
lucidBlocksToolsAndWeapons.intro: Launch rankings reward safe range, fast special cycles, and reliable boss pressure. Build one farm weapon plus one burst answer.

items.name: Metal Wing Boomerang
items.description: Top farming and story weapon with huge coverage, fast special tempo, and safe ranged control.

items.name: Shiverstick
items.description: Most universal redraw option thanks to freeze control, long range safety, and smooth auto performance.

items.name: Metal Slime Sword
items.description: Premium reroll keep with metal-killer value, fast chain pressure, and strong single-target pacing.

items.name: Fire Blade
items.description: Direct burst option that stays useful for boss windows and simple high-damage rotations.

items.name: Crystal Claws
items.description: Excellent close-range burst and co-op DPS ceiling when you can maintain melee uptime.

items.name: Falcon Blade
items.description: Strong wind burst profile with mobility utility that keeps repeat clears fast and consistent.

items.name: Queen's Whip
items.description: Caster-friendly utility pick that supports attack-speed setups and staff-centric team tempos.

items.name: Platinum Plumes
items.description: Spell-seal utility gives it niche value in caster-heavy stages and controlled ranged clears.

lucidBlocksStorageAndInventory.title: Dragon Quest Smash Grow Best Classes
lucidBlocksStorageAndInventory.intro: Class strength at launch follows weapon synergy and positional safety. Thief and Mage are the easiest all-mode winners.

solutions.name: Thief
solutions.description: Best all-purpose launch class with top boomerang and sword synergy plus strong movement tempo for farming routes.

solutions.name: Mage
solutions.description: Most stable ranged caster path with Shiverstick and Queen's Whip synergy in story and farming loops.

solutions.name: Warrior
solutions.description: Reliable melee anchor when you need durable pressure and safer boss attempts with sword and spear options.

solutions.name: Martial Artist
solutions.description: High close-range ceiling with crit-driven damage, rewarding active piloting and aggressive windows.

solutions.name: Priest
solutions.description: Best used for healing security and auto stability while your primary damage comes from another class.

managementTips: Pair class picks with your best 3-star weapon pool instead of forcing weak off-class setups.
Use Thief or Mage as your default progression anchor for smooth launch pacing.
Swap Warrior or Priest in when survival checks become the bottleneck.
Build one clear role per slot to avoid diluted team identity in harder runs.

lucidBlocksQualiaAndBaseBuilding.title: Dragon Quest Smash Grow Retry Gacha
lucidBlocksQualiaAndBaseBuilding.subtitle: Use the retry ticket to patch the gap left after reroll. Ice Staff is the safest all-purpose prize, while Fire Sword and Crystal Claws are the main boss-focused alternatives.
lucidBlocksQualiaAndBaseBuilding.intro: The retry gacha is a launch-only account fixer, not a replacement for reroll. The strongest route is to finish reroll first, secure launch-limited standouts such as Metal Slime Sword or Metal Wing Boomerang, and then use the retry ticket for the weapon role your account still lacks.

items.details: The top retry target. It gives safe ranged damage, strong control, and enough stability to stay useful from early story pushing to harder no-death style missions.
The main runner-up if your reroll already covered crowd clearing. It is favored for boss fights and cleaner single-target pressure.
A fast, high single-target option that works well when your reroll already gave you safer range or AoE. It is one of the two main second-place picks alongside Fire Sword.

Chase launch-limited prizes such as Metal Slime Sword or Metal Wing Boomerang in reroll first, then spend the retry ticket on the missing role. Taking a new 3-star weapon is generally better than forcing a duplicate.
One ticket is awarded right after the tutorial. The second ticket comes from clearing panel mission page 2, so it arrives later than the first one.

The retry pool contains only seven premium 3-star weapons at roughly 14.28571% each and no armor. You can keep rerolling until the exact role you want appears, but once you confirm a result you cannot roll it back.

lucidBlocksWorldRegions.title: Dragon Quest Smash Grow Gacha Guide
lucidBlocksWorldRegions.subtitle: The Metal banner is the launch-era priority because it adds Metal Slime Sword and Metal Wing Boomerang on top of the normal premium pool. Free players should also clear every guaranteed or ad-based pull before spending gems elsewhere.
lucidBlocksWorldRegions.intro: Right now the cleanest spending plan is simple: target the Metal Equipment banner first, use the retryable guaranteed 3-star ticket to fill a missing role, and never skip the daily ad pull. For gem spending, pickup pity sits at 200 pulls and planned 10-pulls are better than random singles.

items.details: The strongest launch banner. Its featured lineup includes Metal Slime Sword and Metal Wing Boomerang, weapons gain +20 damage against metal enemies, and the armor pieces carry all-element resistance.
items.pullRule: Make this your first stop for gems and exchange tickets.

items.details: A guaranteed 3-star weapon banner that can be rerolled over and over until you lock in the result. It is one of the highest-value free power spikes at launch.
items.pullRule: Use it as soon as you know which role your roster still needs.

items.details: A standard banner with one free ad pull per day and a chance to drop 3-star gear. It is easy long-term value and should not be skipped.
items.pullRule: Always take the daily free pull before spending gems.

items.details: A paid-only 10-pull that guarantees one 3-star weapon. It is solid if you are spending, but it still trails the Metal banner for pure launch value.
items.pullRule: Reasonable for spenders after the Metal banner is covered.

items.details: Usable, but weaker than the current pickup value. Treat it as a free-pull sink instead of a primary gem target.
items.pullRule: Use free pulls only; do not make it your main banner.

items.details: Pickup weapons have a 200-pull pity, and planned 10-pulls are favored over scattering singles. This matters most if you are saving for a focused banner instead of spending casually.
items.pullRule: Save deep pulls for a banner you actually want to finish.

lucidBlocksCreaturesAndEnemies.title: Dragon Quest Smash Grow Best Party
lucidBlocksCreaturesAndEnemies.subtitle: The strongest early launch teams split into two clear lanes: a safe Mage-led general farming build and a Thief-led metal farming build. For harder content, stability comes from one anchor weapon, one Warrior slot, and flexible support weapons with strong start-of-run skills.
lucidBlocksCreaturesAndEnemies.intro: This game is easier to build around weapons than around jobs in the abstract. The best practice is to choose one anchor weapon-class pair first, then fill the other two slots with roles that add strong Adventure Skills, cover weak matchups, and keep your runs stable in auto or manual play.

items.title: Auto General Farming Party
items.bestFor: Story pushing, general quest farming, and safe auto play.
notes: Built around Ice Staff range and control.
Fire Sword and Crystal Claws add strong start-of-run skill support.
This setup works as a standard all-purpose farming baseline.

items.title: Metal Farming Party
items.bestFor: Metal enemy hunting and faster early experience farming.
notes: Metal Wing Boomerang is the core anti-metal weapon for this build.
Ice Staff and Crystal Claws work as flexible side slots around the metal core.
Metal Slime Sword is a valid melee main alternative if you prefer a closer-range route.

items.title: Hard-Stage Stability Core
party: Anchor 1: Pick one main weapon-class pair first.
Anchor 2: Choose sub slots by their start-of-run Adventure Skills.
Anchor 3: Keep one Warrior in the roster for Big Shield damage reduction.
Anchor 4: Reclass the early Priest into Thief once job change opens if you need more offense.
items.bestFor: Gauntlet, Shrines, and harder manual fights where consistency matters more than raw speed.
notes: Job change unlocks at Adventure Rank 7.
Job skill panel progress starts at Adventure Rank 8.
Launch-era advice favors wider damage coverage over forcing a healer slot.

lucidBlocksMobilityGear.title: Dragon Quest Smash Grow Release Date
lucidBlocksMobilityGear.subtitle: Dragon Quest Smash/Grow has already launched on iOS and Android as a free-to-play roguelite RPG. The most useful timeline points are the closed beta window, launch-date confirmation, pre-download start, service launch, and the two separate reward deadlines players can still track.
lucidBlocksMobilityGear.intro: This module is built for latecomers who want the launch story in one scan. It covers when testing happened, when the release date was locked in, when pre-download started, when service opened, and how long the launch gifts and event rewards remain active.

items.label: Closed Beta
items.title: The global CBT cycle was scheduled
items.description: Closed beta applications opened on September 17, 2025, ran through October 2, and the test period was planned for October 14 to October 21 with a 30,000-player cap.

items.label: Release Date
items.title: Launch was locked for April 21, 2026
items.description: KLab and Square Enix formally confirmed the launch date for Tuesday, April 21, 2026 UTC and pointed players to the official stores and channels for final launch prep.

items.label: Pre-download
items.title: Pre-download started one day before launch
items.description: Players could install the app on iOS and Android ahead of service start. At that point the service date was already fixed for April 21.

items.label: Launch
items.title: Official service began
items.description: DRAGON QUEST Smash/Grow launched worldwide on iOS and Android as a free-to-play roguelite RPG with in-app purchases.

items.label: Event Deadline
items.title: The first release-event window ends
items.description: The launch login bonus worth up to 1,000 Gems and the release medal event both run through May 5, 2026 at 11:59 JST.

items.label: Gift Deadline
items.title: The early-start 10-pull gift expires
items.description: Players who start early can still claim 10 premium equipment vouchers through October 18, 2026. Tutorial completion also unlocks a retryable guaranteed 3-star weapon ticket for account setup.

lucidBlocksFarmingAndGrowth.title: Dragon Quest Smash Grow Codes, Redemption Status, and Official Drop Channels
lucidBlocksFarmingAndGrowth.subtitle: This page tracks every active code, every expired code, and every official place where new freebies are posted. It is the fastest way to check the live code state without digging through social feeds.
lucidBlocksFarmingAndGrowth.intro: The current tracked code status is simple: there are no active codes and no expired codes listed yet. What matters now is having the live status, the redemption note, and the official watchlist in one place so players can jump on the first real code drop as soon as it appears.

items.title: Active Codes
items.description: The tracked code pages do not list any working Dragon Quest Smash Grow codes at the moment.

items.title: Expired Codes
items.description: No expired codes are listed yet, so the expired archive is still empty.

items.title: How to Redeem
items.description: The tracked code pages have not posted a public in-game redemption flow yet, so this slot works as the live tracker for the first confirmed redeem path.

items.title: Official X
items.description: Use the official English account for launch posts, campaign notices, and future code drops.

items.title: Official Facebook
items.description: Use the official Facebook page for event posts, launch promotions, and reward announcements.

items.title: Official Discord
items.description: The official teaser site links directly to the Dragon Quest Discord, making it part of the main code-watch list.

lucidBlocksBestEarlyUnlocks.title: Dragon Quest Smash Grow Story Walkthrough by Chapter, Unlock, and Boss Check
lucidBlocksBestEarlyUnlocks.subtitle: This module breaks the story route into practical checkpoints so players know what to push first, what each milestone unlocks, and where the first real wall fights appear. It is built for users searching chapter clears, boss help, and early progression order.
lucidBlocksBestEarlyUnlocks.intro: The story route is more than a chapter list because it also gates companions, Hard Mode, Minimal Expedition, and several major account systems. The cleanest early plan is to push through Chapter 1 fast, learn the first element checks at 1-9 and 1-10, then use the early Chapter 2 stretch to stabilize memory choices and unlocks.

sections.summary: Ruina Plains runs from Lv.1 to Lv.10 and becomes your first real element check at 1-9 and 1-10, where Thunder and Wind are the recommended answers.
notes: Stage 1-5 is the first major story checkpoint because your first companion joins here.
Ruina Plains 1-5 and 1-6 are also the standout early Lime Slime memory farming spots.
Clearing 1-10 unlocks both Minimal Expedition and Story Hard.

sections.summary: The early walls are not spread evenly. The first real trouble points are the heal check at 1-5, the cast check at 1-6, and the element checks at 1-9 and 1-10.

rows.tip: Clear the smaller enemies before the heal lands so the fight does not drag.
Back off as soon as the casting effect appears and reset your angle before re-engaging.
Prioritize dodging the scattered rock attacks and bring Thunder or Wind if your box already supports it.
Use Thunder or Wind and let Auto handle the movement if manual dodges are costing too much HP.

notes: The game starts asking for cleaner movement and stronger element alignment at the very end of Chapter 1.
Do not overfeed useful memories before this stretch because the right early memory matters more than small raw stat bumps.

sections.summary: Chapter 2 is where the story starts paying off with better farming habits, more party flexibility, and stronger account management decisions.

rows.details: Monster Rush opens after clearing 1-10 and moving into Chapter 2.
Push to 2-2 quickly because the second companion is one of the biggest early power spikes for party flexibility.
Rare monsters can appear before the boss wave, so scan the room instead of sprinting straight to the portal.
Keep rare memories and match memory color to slot color instead of burning strong early pieces as enhancement fodder.

notes: The early Chapter 2 goal is not only story progress but also locking in better long-run farming habits.
Color-matched memories are much stronger than random off-color fills, so this is where the account starts to feel more structured.

sections.section: Key story unlocks to plan around
sections.summary: The story is the main feature gate for the account, so every chapter milestone has a systems payoff attached to it.

rows.trigger: Clear all stages in a chapter

notes: If a player is stuck deciding between farming and pushing story, these unlocks are the reason to keep advancing.
Minimal Expedition is especially important because it starts feeding passive upgrade materials and memories.

lucidBlocksAchievementTracker.title: Dragon Quest Smash Grow Best Memory Tier List, Color Matching, and Early Farm Targets
lucidBlocksAchievementTracker.subtitle: Memory is one of the most important long-term power systems in Dragon Quest Smash Grow, so the best picks are the ones that combine strong effects, strong stat value, and clean slot fit. Color matching matters because matching a memory to the slot color boosts its stats by 1.2x.
lucidBlocksAchievementTracker.intro: The strongest memory is not always the rarest one in a vacuum. Warrior-style setups care a lot about yellow-friendly slot value, Mage pages naturally lean into purple, and every build gets more efficient once the memory effect and slot color are both working for the same plan.

tiers.summary: These are the premium memories that combine high stats with broad build value and stay useful deep into progression.

entries.name: Fire Warrior
entries.summary: A top-end physical memory with high stat value and direct fire physical support, making it strong even before a box is fully optimized.
entries.best_for: Fire physical lineups and general attacker slots
entries.slot_tip: Match the slot color first, then let the fire physical boost carry the rest of the value.

entries.name: Killer Panther
entries.summary: One of the cleanest all-purpose memories because it brings high stats and boosts special-move gauge gain.
entries.best_for: General progression teams and fast special rotations
entries.slot_tip: This is the easiest SS memory to slot when raw value matters more than a narrow build tag.

entries.name: Archfiend
entries.summary: A sword-build premium memory with high stats and doubled sword count, giving sword-tag runs much more upside.
entries.slot_tip: Its value spikes hardest when the run already wants sword-tag adventure skills.

entries.name: King Slime
entries.summary: A premium pebble memory with high stats and doubled pebble count, making it the strongest top-end enabler for pebble packages.
entries.slot_tip: Use it when the weapon and run path already reward pebble scaling instead of generic filler.

tiers.summary: These memories are strong enough to shape real builds and are often easier to use early than a narrow SS piece.

entries.name: Lime Slime
entries.summary: A standout early magic memory with strong spell-side value and a built-in Arrow Boost (Slow) starter. It is one of the most important early purple memories in the game.
entries.slot_tip: Purple slots get the cleanest payoff here, which lines up naturally with Mage-style pages.

entries.name: Mini Demon
entries.summary: A strong low-cost attacker memory with good stats and a Sword Boost (Heal) starter that adds practical sustain.
entries.slot_tip: A great bridge piece when you want offense and small survivability in the same slot.

entries.name: Yeti
entries.summary: A direct sword-type damage memory that fits neatly into sword-tag loadouts without demanding a full late-game box.
entries.slot_tip: This is strongest once the weapon package is already leaning into sword tags.

entries.name: Ice Golem
entries.summary: A strong magic-side option with high magic attack and light spell damage support.
entries.slot_tip: A clean second step after Lime Slime for players building real spell pages.

entries.name: Blood Hand
entries.summary: A focused pebble damage upgrade that gives pebble-based weapons a very noticeable damage floor.
entries.slot_tip: Use it under King Slime as the more practical build-specific upgrade.

entries.name: Slime Knight
entries.summary: Its quest-start +1 Great Arrow effect gives immediate tempo and makes early projectile setups feel smoother.
entries.best_for: Arrow-leaning openers and faster first-room clears
entries.slot_tip: This is the easiest way to improve early run tempo before the box is full of premium options.

tiers.summary: These are the first practical memory targets to repeat while the account is still growing and story progress is still the main priority.

entries.name: Lime Slime
entries.summary: The best early Chapter 1 target because it is both useful and realistic to farm while pushing story.
entries.slot_tip: Its purple color makes it especially valuable on Mage-style slot pages.

entries.name: Drackyma
entries.summary: A clean early physical filler that boosts wind physical damage and is easy to pick up before deeper farming routes open.
entries.slot_tip: Use it as an efficient bridge piece while chasing stronger long-term memory pages.

entries.name: Stone Man
entries.summary: A low-cost early memory with a simple physical damage bonus that fits many early physical pages.
entries.slot_tip: This is the easiest way to add a clean physical bump during late Chapter 1.

entries.name: Smile Rock
entries.summary: A practical Chapter 2 target because the routes around it also overlap with other useful memory drops.
entries.best_for: Earth-leaning builds and efficient repeat farming
entries.slot_tip: Best used when you want one repeatable chapter stretch to produce multiple useful drops.

lucidBlocksSingleplayerAndPlatformFAQ.title: Dragon Quest Smash Grow Adventure Skills Builds, Core Picks, and Weapon Pairings
lucidBlocksSingleplayerAndPlatformFAQ.subtitle: Blessings and Adventure Skills are the heart of the roguelite loop, so this module focuses on the strongest build directions instead of dumping a flat skill list. The current meta starts with universal normal-attack power, then branches into sword or Jibaria packages when the run gives the right tags.
lucidBlocksSingleplayerAndPlatformFAQ.intro: Adventure Skills appear during the run through Grow crystals and other in-run picks, so the best build is the one that converts random offers into a clean damage package. Right now the most stable route is still normal-attack scaling, while sword and Jibaria builds take over once the correct support pieces start chaining together.

items.name: Normal Attack Core
items.summary: This is the most stable all-purpose build in the game and the best default route when a run offers mixed choices.
items.when_to_pick: Use this as the default line for story pushes, challenge farming, and any run that does not commit early to a narrower build.

items.name: Sword Build
items.summary: The cleanest build-specific damage route once sword-tag pieces start showing up and the run is clearly heading in that direction.
items.when_to_pick: Commit once early sword pieces appear or when the team already starts the run with sword support.

items.name: Jibaria Build
items.summary: A powerful mob-clearing route that fixes the screen-control problems of short-range weapons and covers dense rooms very efficiently.
items.when_to_pick: Use this when the account is leaning on short-range weapons and needs safer room control.

items.name: Early Bagi Ball Opener
items.summary: This is one of the strongest early stabilizers because it adds rotating wind damage around the character and scales quickly.
items.when_to_pick: Grab it early when the normal-attack package is still incomplete and the run needs easier room control.

items.name: Emergency Utility Package
items.summary: Not every run needs more damage. These utility cards are the clean fix when the party is losing on sustain or special timing.
items.when_to_pick: Take utility after the run already has enough damage but is still failing on survival, recovery, or special-gauge timing.

lucidBlocksSteamDeckAndController.eyebrow: Dragon Quest Smash Grow Resource Route
lucidBlocksSteamDeckAndController.title: Dragon Quest Smash Grow Gems Farming: Best Ways to Farm Gems Early and Long Term
lucidBlocksSteamDeckAndController.subtitle: Your fastest early gems come from uncleared Adventure stages, then the route shifts into daily, rank, and collection rewards. This module keeps the burst sources and the passive sources in one place.
lucidBlocksSteamDeckAndController.intro: If you want more pulls without paying, start with one-time clears and then lock into the repeatable routine. The best pattern is simple: push new stages for a fast gem burst, then stack missions, Adventure Rank rewards, codex progress, and panel missions for long-term income.

items.title: Push uncleared Adventure stages first
items.description: Uncleared Adventure stages are the fastest early gem source. First-clear rewards plus stage mission rewards usually add up to about 100 to 200 gems per stage.

items.title: Keep the login bonus streak going
items.description: Login bonuses are a steady passive source of gems. During active campaigns, event login rewards stack on top of the normal daily login flow.

items.title: Finish daily and weekly missions
items.description: Missions are the core long-term gem route once your easy story clears slow down. Daily missions give 30 gems per day, and the weekly track gives 300 gems once you reach 100 points.

items.title: Raise Adventure Rank while you progress
items.description: Adventure Rank rewards hand out gems again and again as you climb. Many reward milestones pay 500 gems, and the full reward track up to Rank 50 totals 10,000 gems.

items.title: Fill the Memory and Monster Codex
items.description: At Adventure Rank 2, the Memory Codex and Monster Codex unlock. Filling codex points adds another gem source on top of the stat bonuses you get from collection progress.

items.title: Clear Rookie and Panel Missions whenever you stall
items.description: The Rookie and Panel mission tracks pay out gems, tickets, and upgrade materials, and the mission pages do not expire. They are a reliable side route whenever your main story push slows down.
highlights: Good backup route between story pushes

lucidBlocksSettingsAndAccessibility.eyebrow: Dragon Quest Smash Grow Daily Routine
lucidBlocksSettingsAndAccessibility.title: Dragon Quest Smash Grow Daily Checklist: Fast Daily Reset Route
lucidBlocksSettingsAndAccessibility.subtitle: This is the compact version of what to do every day so stamina, free pulls, and exchange freebies never go to waste. It is built for a quick login-to-logoff loop.
lucidBlocksSettingsAndAccessibility.intro: When you only have a few minutes, do the checklist in this order. It covers every daily action that turns into gems, tickets, stamina value, or passive materials without asking you to overthink your route.

items.title: Claim all login bonuses
items.description: Take the regular login reward on your first login of the day. During events, claim the event login reward too so you do not miss stacked freebies.

items.title: Finish daily missions
items.description: Daily missions refresh every day and pay gold, weapon upgrade stones, and gems. They are one of the easiest checks to finish in a short session.

items.title: Use your stage skips
items.description: Any stage you have already cleared can be skipped from the sortie screen. Use the skip function on the material or medal stage you currently need most.

items.title: Spend natural stamina before it caps
items.description: Stamina refills over time, so do not let it sit full. Story, Growth Quests, and other farming stages all work as good sinks.

items.title: Do the free daily pull
items.description: You can do one free gacha pull per day by watching an ad. It is a small habit, but it slowly grows your weapon and gear pool for free.

items.title: Clear the free shop items
items.description: The free daily exchange includes up to 2 Multiplayer Passes and 1 Shrine Key from Gold Exchange, plus 1 Metal Ticket from Item Purchase after an ad. The free stock refreshes at 5:00.

items.title: Dispatch the idle expedition squad
items.description: After Chapter 1 Epilogue, the idle expedition system unlocks. Keep it running so real-time item collection never stops while you are offline.

lucidBlocksUpdatesAndPatchNotes.eyebrow: Dragon Quest Smash Grow Power Growth
lucidBlocksUpdatesAndPatchNotes.title: Dragon Quest Smash Grow Leveling Guide: Level Fast Without Wasting Tickets
lucidBlocksUpdatesAndPatchNotes.subtitle: Fast leveling in Smash/Grow is built around Metal Quests, Adventure Rank unlocks, and smart party setup. The goal is not only to gain levels, but to unlock the systems that keep your power moving.
lucidBlocksUpdatesAndPatchNotes.intro: The clean route is to unlock the right content first, spend Metal Tickets well, and only fall back to story grinding when your ticket stock runs out. Levels are strong on their own, but the real payoff is how they connect to Adventure Rank, job progression, and Memory cost.

items.title: Rush Adventure Rank 4 for Growth Quests
items.description: Adventure Rank 4 unlocks Growth Quests, which include high-value growth content such as the Metal Dungeon and Gold Quest. That is the first major breakpoint for serious leveling.

items.title: Spend Metal Tickets on Metal Quests
items.description: Metal Quests are the best direct EXP route because each run gives a large amount of experience at once. They require special tickets, so treat them as your main burst-leveling resource.

items.title: Claim free and rank-based ticket sources
items.description: You can get 1 Metal Ticket per day from the Item Purchase exchange after watching an ad, and more Metal Tickets or Metal Brothers Tickets come from Adventure Rank rewards.

items.title: Use metal-slayer weapons if you have them
items.description: Metal Wing and Metal Slime Sword are excellent for Metal farming because their specials deal 270% neutral physical damage with +20 against Metal enemies. If you own one, your leveling speed jumps hard.

items.title: Push story when you run out of tickets
items.description: If your Metal Ticket stock is empty, keep moving through story stages instead of stalling. Story progress helps character growth while also pushing Adventure Rank, unlocks, and first-clear rewards.

items.title: Level with the right party rules in mind
items.description: Levels are tracked by vocation, so changing vocation sends that job back to Level 1. When power-leveling, bring at least one high-level ally so the run stays stable while your lower-level job catches up.
highlights: Bring at least one high-level ally

lucidBlocksCrashFixAndTroubleshooting.title: Dragon Quest Smash Grow Co-Op Guide: How Co-Op Works Right Now
lucidBlocksCrashFixAndTroubleshooting.subtitle: Smash/Grow co-op is built around four-player runs, room numbers, and limited entry items. This module focuses on how to join, how to host, and what restrictions matter before you queue.
lucidBlocksCrashFixAndTroubleshooting.intro: The important thing to know is that co-op is easy to enter once you understand the room system, but it is not built around a full social layer. Treat it like a fast mission mode with shared room codes, limited entry items, and sticker-based communication.

items.title: Co-op is a 4-player mode
items.description: Officially, Co-Op Quests let you team up with up to 3 other players. That means one host plus three additional players in a full room.

items.title: You can join two ways
items.description: The game supports random matchmaking and room-number matching. If you already have a code, the room-number option is the cleanest way to jump into the correct run.

items.title: Private rooms use a room number
items.description: Creating a room generates a private room number. This is the best setup for friends, Discord groups, or recruitment posts because everyone can enter the exact same room.

items.title: Each entry spends a Multiplayer Pass
items.description: Co-op runs consume an item called a Multiplayer Pass when you enter. Free passes can be exchanged from Gold Exchange up to 2 times per day, and free-pass storage caps at 20.

items.title: There is no friend system
items.description: Smash/Grow currently has no friend list, so there is no direct invite flow. In practice, co-op is organized through random matching or by sharing a room number.

items.title: Communication is sticker-only
items.description: In-game co-op communication is limited to stamps. It works for quick signals, but it is not built for detailed calls or role assignments.

items.title: Use co-op for repeat medal farming once your team is ready
items.description: Multiplayer Quests can drop current medal rewards such as Release Commemoration Medals, so co-op is a good place to spend passes once your story team is stable enough to clear runs cleanly.

faq.title: Dragon Quest Smash Grow
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Dragon Quest Smash Grow.

questions.question: What is Dragon Quest Smash Grow about?
questions.answer: Dragon Quest Smash Grow is a mobile roguelite RPG focused on fast combat loops, weapon progression, class routing, and co-op quests.

questions.question: How should Dragon Quest Smash Grow players start progression?
questions.answer: Begin with a structured reroll route, lock a core weapon pair, and push story unlocks before broad side farming.

questions.question: Which Dragon Quest Smash Grow classes are easiest at launch?
questions.answer: Thief and Mage are the most consistent launch anchors due to weapon synergy, safety, and smooth farming pace.

questions.question: How can Dragon Quest Smash Grow players improve gacha value?
questions.answer: Use guaranteed redraw strategically, track codes and events, and keep gem reserves for planned banner windows.

questions.question: Does Dragon Quest Smash Grow include co-op content?
questions.answer: Yes. Dragon Quest Smash Grow supports up to four-player co-op with role-based team coordination and repeatable quest rewards.

questions.question: Where can Dragon Quest Smash Grow be downloaded?
questions.answer: Dragon Quest Smash Grow is available on the App Store and Google Play through official platform listings.
